course_title: Foundations of Physiotherapy III: Practice & Management
course_description:
This course provides fundamental knowledge and understanding to assess and manage client conditions involving pathology, injury and pain of the neuromusculoskeletal system. The course is based on a client-centred framework and uses an interprofessional teamwork approach. As a foundation for comprehensive and informed client assessment and management, the course covers: 1) foundations of neuromusculoskeletal physiotherapy (e.g. mobility aids; orthoses; tissue pathology and repair processes; pain; the association of the skeletal and neuromuscular systems with pain, injury and pathology); and 2) the application of foundations to physiotherapy and medical management of clients with specific neuromusculoskeletal conditions (e.g. rheumatological disorders, orthopaedic conditions, vascular conditions, amputation, burn injury, spinal cord injury).

This course provides the student with fundamental knowledge and understanding to assess and manage conditions involving neuromusculoskeletal pathology, injury and pain. PHTY7815 is based on a client-centred evidence-based practice framework, with reference to the biopsychosocial International Classification of Functioning, Disability and Health (ICF) model. The course uses an interprofessional approach to client assessment and management, and introduces occupational health and safety considerations important for physiotherapy practice.

As a foundation for comprehensive and informed client assessment and management, ﾠthe course covers: 1) foundational knowledge and skills of neuromusculoskeletal physiotherapy (e.g. mobility aids; orthoses; tissue pathology and repair processes; pain); and 2) the application of foundational knowledge and skills to the clinical care of clients with specific neuromusculoskeletal conditions (e.g. rheumatological disorders, orthopaedic conditions, vascular conditions, amputation, burn injury, spinal cord injury).

course_aims:
This course has three key aims:

1. Provide foundational knowledge required for physiotherapy assessment and management of clients with neuromusculoskeletal pathology, injury and pain;

2. Apply foundational knowledge to clients with specific neuromusculoskeletal conditions, across inpatient and outpatient settings; and

3. Facilitate integration of foundational and applied knowledge in developing holistic assessment and management of clients with neuromusculoskeletal pathology, injury and pain.

learning_outcomes.description: Explain tissue pathology and wound healing mechanisms, and their relevance to the assessment and management of clients with orthopaedic, rheumatological, vascular, and neurological conditions.

Explain pain mechanisms, and identify and apply pain classifications, to inform appropriate assessment and management of clients with orthopaedic, rheumatological, vascular, and neurological conditions.
Describe a range of orthopaedic, rheumatological, vascular, and neurological conditions in terms of their pathophysiology, clinical presentation, and evidence-based, biopsychosocial management.
Develop and justify safe, effective and client-centred physiotherapy assessments and management plans for clients with a range of orthopaedic, rheumatological, vascular, and neurological conditions.

Safely perform and document physiotherapy assessments and interventions for clients with a range of orthopaedic, rheumatological, vascular, and neurological conditions, demonstrating clinical reasoning and adherence to professional standards.

Apply principles of mobility aid and orthoses prescription to clients with a range of orthopaedic, rheumatological, vascular, and neurological conditions, tailoring interventions to individual needs and current evidence.
Explain and exemplify the role of the physiotherapist within an interdisciplinary healthcare team, with emphasis on collaboration and professional responsibility.
Demonstrate clear, respectful, and responsive communication with clients, carers, and other health professionals to enhance interprofessional collaboration and client-centred care.

Integrate knowledge and clinical reasoning to manage clients with multiple neuromusculoskeletal conditions, applying a holistic, client-centred approach in the context of clinical complexity and uncertainty.

assessment_details.title: In-semester written exam
assessment_details.other_conditions: Time limited, Secure. See the conditions definitions
assessment_details.task_description: The in-semester written examination will consist of multiple choice and short answer questions to test your knowledge (and application of knowledge) on foundations of neuromusculoskeletal physiotherapy. This exam will examine all work covered in weeks 1-5 (inclusive) of semester. It will be an invigilated, on campus, closed book exam. Feedback : An overall summary of feedback will be provided, including cohort feedback on areas for further development. This will be released after deferred examinations are complete. Requests for further feedback must be submitted in writing to the course coordinator, and must include a reflection on exam performance and specific questions for feedback. Use of AI technology : This assessment task is to be completed in-person. The use of generative Artificial Intelligence (AI) or Machine Translation (MT) tools will not be permitted. Any attempted use of AI or MT may constitute student misconduct under the Student Code of Conduct. Please see the Assessment tab on Blackboard for information this assessment item. Read full task description Hide full task description

Deferral or extension
You may be able to defer this exam .
Deferred exams can be requested for this assessment item. Please apply for a deferred exam if there are exceptional or unavoidable circumstances, or as a one-off discretionary request. Deferred exam requests should be submitted as soon as possible, and no later than five calendar days after the date of the original exam. Further details, including how to apply, can be found at - Deferring an exam . Please also email the Course Coordinator to advise of the deferred exam request. Please select "In-Semester" exam when you apply for the deferred exam on SI-net.

learning_resources.library_resources: Find the required and recommended resources for this course on the UQ Library website.

learning_activities.learning_period: Multiple weeks From O-week To Week 13 (16 Feb - 31 May)
learning_activities.topic: Lectures Lectures will be delivered by expert lecturers from within the School of Health & Rehabilitation Sciences and The University of Queensland, as well as invited guest lecturers from a variety of health professions. Content will be delivered via a combination of pre-recorded online lectures and live in-person lectures as indicated on Blackboard under the course schedule, with up to 4 hours of lecture activity planned each week. Lectures will provide foundational information across a range of topics, including mobility aids; orthoses; tissue pathology and repair processes; pain; and the assessment and management of clients with specific neuromusculoskeletal conditions (e.g. rheumatological disorders, orthopaedic conditions, vascular conditions, amputations, burn injury, spinal cord injury).

learning_activities.learning_period: Multiple weeks From Week 1 To Week 13 (23 Feb - 31 May)
learning_activities.topic: Practical Classes (On campus) A diverse range of learning activities are scheduled for the Friday practical classes. These practical classes will run for 1.5 hours each, and will be delivered by experts from within The University of Queensland as well as invited guest lecturers from a variety of industry settings. Content will cover fundamental physiotherapy assessment and management skills (e.g. mobility aid prescription, neurovascular assessments), as well as assessment and management of individuals with specific neuromusculoskeletal conditions (e.g. orthopaedics, spinal cord injuries, amputations). To prepare effectively, participants must complete the associated preparatory tasks prior to attending (e.g. listen to relevant lectures, watch corresponding skills videos). As the practical classes are an interactive experience, they will not be recorded.

learning_activities.learning_period: Multiple weeks From Week 1 To Week 13 (23 Feb - 31 May)
learning_activities.topic: Skills videos Skills videos provide you with demonstrations and explanations of the some of the key skills that you will be practicing in the practical classes. These videos are designed to supplement your learning, and it is expected that you will watch the videos prior to attending the associated practical session. Please refer to Blackboard for more details.

Practical Classes (Off campus) A once-off off-campus observational practical class at Princess Alexandra Hospital is scheduled in Week 7 (Physiotherapy management of patients with amputations); all students must meet the specified requirements listed on Blackboard and communicated by UQ to attend. Please see Blackboard for more information.
